coredata.dc:title: One-step preparation of MnO2 nanozyme by PS-CDs for antibacterial, inhibition of S. aureus biofilm growth and colorimetric assay of tiopronin
coredata.prism:publicationName: Journal of Industrial and Engineering Chemistry
coredata.prism:copyright: © 2023 The Korean Society of Industrial and Engineering Chemistry. Published by Elsevier B.V. All rights reserved.
coredata.prism:publisher: The Korean Society of Industrial and Engineering Chemistry. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.dc:creator: Liu, Huan
Zhao, Dan
Zhang, Changpeng
Li, Minghao
Zhang, Shijie
Xiao, Xincai
coredata.dc:description: The novel nanozyme has been used in the detection field as biosensor, but has also been employed in new fields like antibacterial and inhibiting of biofilm growth applications. This paper reports the rapid preparation of the nanozyme PS-CDs-MnO2 with oxidase activity by using PS-CDs as reducing agent to reduce KMnO4. The mechanisms of positive electrical property of PS-CDs-MnO2 and the single linear oxygen (1O2) have promised the broad-spectrum antibacterial ability and high efficiency in eliminating bacteria, with 48 h long-term antibacterial effect. The minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) against Staphylococcus aureus (S. aureus), Bacillus subtilis (B. subtilis), Escherichia coli (E. coli) and Pseudomonas aeruginosa (P. aeruginosa) at 7.50 μg/mL, 60 μg/mL, 15 μg/mL and 60 μg/mL, respectively. The prepared PS-CDs-MnO2 could effectively inhibit the formation of S. aureus biofilm at the concentration of 10 μg/mL. The visually sensitive and selective sensing platform based on PS-CDs-MnO2-TMB was established, and realized the colorimetric detection of tiopronin (TPN) with a detection range at 285.71 nM–14.28 μM and detection limit at 6.30 nM. This research provides a good theoretical and practical basis for the preparation of oxidase nanozyme and its application in detection, antibacterial and inhibition of biofilm formation fields.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Nanozyme
Manganese dioxide
Carbon dots
Antibacterial and biofilm growth inhibition
Colorimetric detection
Tiopronin

The study on the antibacterial and biofilm-inhibiting ability of PS-CDs-MnO2
The study on the antibacterial ability of PS-CDs-MnO2
MICs against different strains of bacteria
The long-term antibacterial ability of PS-CDs-MnO2 against S. aureus
The inhibition ability of PS-CDs-MnO2 against S. aureus biofilm growth
The pH-dependency of enzyme activity of PS-CDs-MnO2

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: (a) UV–Vis spectra of PS-CDs-MnO2, PS-CDs, TMB and PS-CDs-MnO2, TMB and PS-CDs, TMB solution only, the illustration shows the corresponding solution, (b) TEM image of PS-CDs-MnO2, (c) EDS image of PS-CDs-MnO2, (d) Cell viability (%) measured by CCK-8 method. L-929 cells were incubated with PS-CDs or PS-CDs-MnO2 at 37 °C for 24 h.
Long-lasting antibacterial curve in 48 h (a) PS-CDs-MnO2, (b) PS-CDs.
Curve of S. aureus biofilm growth inhibition at pH = 7/8 for 48 h (a) pH = 7 PS-CDs-MnO2, (b) pH = 7 PS-CDs, (c) pH = 8 PS-CDs-MnO2, (d) pH = 8 PS-CDs.

(a) Different PBS solutions (containing 2.5 μM SOSG; 2.5 μM SOSG and 1 mg/mL PS-CDs-MnO2; 2.5 μM SOSG and 1 mg/mL PS-CDs), (b) Different PBS solutions (containing 2.5 μM SOSG; 1 mg/mL PS-CDs-MnO2; 1 mg/mL PS-CDs) before and after 30 min of daylight irradiation or placed in the dark environment for 30 min fluorescence intensity changes, SEM images of S. aureus after treatment with (c) PBS and (d) PS-CDs-MnO2 (600 μg/mL).
The absorbance values at 655 nm were measured after incubation with PS-CDs-MnO2 with S. aureus for 0, 1, 2, 4, 6, 8 and 20 h with the addition of TMB.
UV–Vis spectra of PS-CDs-MnO2 + TMB, PS-CDs-MnO2 + TMB + TPN.

(a) Peak intensity variation at 655 nm for PS-CDs-MnO2-TPN-TMB solution, the inset shows the corresponding color change of the solution, and (b) linear relationship between absorbance variation (A655) and TPN concentration ranging from 285.71 nM to 14.28 μM.
Selectivity test of TPN (1.00 × 10−3 M) by adding interfering substances K+, Mn2+, Pb2+, Na+, Cu2+, Zn2 +, Mg2+, Ag+, Ca2+, Hg2+, Fe3+ and Glc.
Schematic illustration of the synthesis of nanozyme PS-CDs-MnO2.
The mechanism of PS-CDs-MnO2 against various bacteria and inhibition of S. aureus biofilm growth.
Comparison of MICs values of different MnO2 nanomaterials against S. aureus and E. coli.
Antibacterial ability of PS-CDs-MnO2 against S. aureus under different pH values.

row.entry: MICs (μg/mL) value at different pH values

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: MICs of PS-CDs-MnO2 on various bacteria.

head.ce:title: One-step preparation of MnO2 nanozyme by PS-CDs for antibacterial, inhibition of S. aureus biofilm growth and colorimetric assay of tiopronin

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Pharmaceutical Sciences, South-Central Minzu University, Wuhan 430074, PR China

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: National Demonstration Center for Experimental Ethnopharmacology Education (South-Central Minzu University), Wuhan 430065, PR China

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: National Demonstration Center for Experimental Ethnopharmacology Education (South-Central Minzu University), Wuhan 430065, PR China

ce:correspondence.ce:text: Corresponding author at: School of Pharmaceutical Sciences, South-Central Minzu University, Wuhan 430074, PR China.

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: The novel nanozyme has been used in the detection field as biosensor, but has also been employed in new fields like antibacterial and inhibiting of biofilm growth applications. This paper reports the rapid preparation of the nanozyme PS-CDs-MnO2 with oxidase activity by using PS-CDs as reducing agent to reduce KMnO4. The mechanisms of positive electrical property of PS-CDs-MnO2 and the single linear oxygen (1O2) have promised the broad-spectrum antibacterial ability and high efficiency in eliminating bacteria, with 48 h long-term antibacterial effect. The minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) against Staphylococcus aureus (S. aureus), Bacillus subtilis (B. subtilis), Escherichia coli (E. coli) and Pseudomonas aeruginosa (P. aeruginosa) at 7.50 μg/mL, 60 μg/mL, 15 μg/mL and 60 μg/mL, respectively. The prepared PS-CDs-MnO2 could effectively inhibit the formation of S. aureus biofilm at the concentration of 10 μg/mL. The visually sensitive and selective sensing platform based on PS-CDs-MnO2-TMB was established, and realized the colorimetric detection of tiopronin (TPN) with a detection range at 285.71 nM–14.28 μM and detection limit at 6.30 nM. This research provides a good theoretical and practical basis for the preparation of oxidase nanozyme and its application in detection, antibacterial and inhibition of biofilm formation fields.

ce:section.ce:para: The drawbacks of natural enzymes, such as low stability and high cost, have severely limited their applications in biomedicine and food safety. With the development of nanotechnology, nanozymes have attracted extensive interests and researches due to their high catalytic efficiency and large-scale production. At the present, nanozyme has gradually replaced natural enzymes in the fields of biosensing, food safety, medical therapy and environmental detection [1–4]. In particular, nanozyme exhibits great potential in antibacterial and treatment of infections due to its excellent catalytic ability.

Since the emergence of antibiotic-resistant bacteria, the search for novel and more effective antibacterial agents has become the focus of the researchers. Currently, nanozymes have attracted much attention in the biomedicine field, especially in antibacterial and inhibition of biofilm growth, and disease treatment, due to its merits such as low toxicity, broad-spectrum antibacterial ability, and high antibacterial efficiency [5–7]. Liao et al. designed a nanozyme prepared by integrating two-dimensional (2D) bimetallic metal–organic frameworks (MOFs) with plasmonic Au nanoparticles (Au NPs), and the product could improve the enzyme properties and treatment in bacterial-caused infections and have wound-healing ability. This nanoparticle realizes its antibacterial ability through localized surface plasmon resonance (LSPR) and charge transfer in bimetallic MOFs [8]. Li and his team have prepared novel gold-manganese oxide nanoparticle (Au-MnOx) nanozyme with a spiky surface , which has photothermal properties and peroxidase-like activity that could achieve combined photothermal therapy (PTT)/chemodynamic therapy (CDT) antimicrobial therapy under NIR-II irradiation [9]. Hao et al. developed an ultra-high activity Fe, N co-doped ultra-thin hollow carbon framework antibacterial nanozyme that can catalyze the generation of H2O2 to produce ·OH, and the highly toxic reactive oxygen species (ROS) including ·OH can effectively kill bacteria as a basis for antimicrobial research [10]. The nanozymes prepared by the above researchers have problems such as cumbersome synthesis process, need for light irradiation, and need for H2O2 participation to have high antibacterial activity which hinder their further development. However, some research team already reported the H2O2/UV advanced oxidation process could eliminate E. coli from the industrial effluent without the presence of nanozyme [11]. So the development of novel nanozyme with simple synthesis route, and high antibacterial efficiency without the requirement of other antibacterial molecules or external light, is more challenging.

Bacterial biofilms are a special type of biological community that create effective physical and chemical barriers to protect embedded bacteria through their own synthesized extracellular polymeric substances (EPS) and generate new resistance mechanisms specific to biofilm-associated bacteria [12], which means that once a biofilm is formed it is difficult to be removed thus leading to further health problems, making the development of new antibacterial agents a more serious challenge. The rapid development of nanozymes offers new opportunities to solve those problems and points the way to the development of alternative antibiotics. Pan et al. prepared chitosan grafted Fe-doped-carbon dots of highly efficient artificial nanozyme (CS@Fe/CDs) for bacterial biofilm eradication [13]. As promising alternatives to antibiotics, oxidase nanocatalysts face bottlenecks of low activity and unclear catalytic mechanisms, which severely limit their development for anti-infective therapy. Therefore, the development of a safe and efficient antibacterial and bacterial biofilm growth-inhibiting nanozyme becomes an urgent problem.

Novel nanozyme material types are abundant and mainly include transition metal oxides, noble metals, organometallic frameworks and carbon-based materials [14]. The metal oxide CuFe2O4, reported by N. Arsalani's group, achieved the catalytic reduction of nitroanilines [15], and further, the magnetic complexes were prepared by co-precipitation method combining carbon quantum dots (CQDs) with CuFe2O4 [16,17], still with good catalytic activity. Manganese dioxide (MnO2) in transition metal oxides is characterized by high catalytic performance, cost effectiveness and good environmental compatibility [18]. Currently, established methods for MnO2 synthesis are sol–gel, chemical coprecipitation, solid-phase, template, microwave, and redox methods [19]. Most of the MnO2 produced by conventional methods is hydrophobic, but water-soluble MnO2 nanoparticles are more valuable for utilization in biochemical sensing and medical applications due to their enhanced catalytic and oxidative activities. The room temperature stirring method of redox is the most widely accepted method for the preparation of water-soluble MnO2 with strong operability, low cost and broad application, and albumin from bovine serum (BSA) is commonly used as the ligand and reducing agent. The exploration of new ligands to make the products with unique biochemical functions is a hot topic of current research. In this paper, we report for the first time the preparation of antimicrobial functional MnO2 nanozymes by a one-step room temperature method using antimicrobial carbon dots (CDs) as reducing agents. CDs, as a novel quasi-spherical carbon nanomaterial, have the advantages of small particle size, low toxicity, good biocompatibility, and adjustable surface oxygen-containing groups (hydroxyl, carbonyl, and carboxyl groups) [20]. In addition, CDs, as strong electron donors, can be designed as novel reducing agents to promote the nucleation of noble metal nanoparticles. CDs can also stabilize metal nanoparticles to prevent their aggregation in pure aqueous solutions due to their excellent surface reactive groups [21]. Therefore, the preparation of novel MnO2 using CDs as reducing agents is very promising.

Biochemical assay is the most promising application of nanozyme. nanozyme possesses multiple enzymatic activities, including peroxidase (POD) activity, catalase (CAT) activity, superoxide dismutase (SOD), and oxidase (OXD) activity [22]. Among them, OXD nanozyme could effectively convert O2 into strongly oxidizing O2 −, and further catalyze colorless 3, 3', 5, 5'-tetramethylbenzidine (TMB) into visible blue oxidized TMB (oxTMB) [23]. Unlike CAT nanozyme [24], OXD nanozyme avoid instability, high toxicity, volatility, and self-decomposing H2O2 in the catalysis process [25], which promises its broader applications. The platform based on the oxidase-TMB system can realize the detection of dozens of substances, such as glutathione [26], ascorbic acid [27], hydroquinone [28], and Hg2+ [29]. Tiopronin (TPN) is a drug used for the treatment of cystinuria, rheumatoid arthritis, liver and age-related cataracts [30], and also used in clinical treatment as an antidote for heavy metal poisoning and as a radioprotective agent [31]. In clinical treatment, the dose of TPN usage is a key issue. An inappropriate dose may cause gastrointestinal upset, oral ulcers, proteinuria, nephrotic syndrome, and myasthenia gravis with various side effects [32]. As a sulfhydryl-containing derivative with similar structure as glutathione, TPN is expected to be visually, easily, and sensitively detected by oxidase-based MnO2-TMB to serve the clinical drug testing applications.

This paper reports the preparation of nanozyme PS-CDs-MnO2 using antibacterial PS-CDs as raw material and reducing agent to reduce KMnO4. The prepared PS-CDs-MnO2 exhibits excellent antibacterial ability and could inhibit the growth of S. aureus biofilm, and the relevant data of the antibacterial ability of prepared PS-CDs-MnO2 have been compared with those of other MnO2 antibacterial nanomaterials, as shown in Table 1 . A series of characterization tools were used to investigate the optical properties, surface groups, and elemental components of the product. In addition, the prepared PS-CDs-MnO2 also exhibits relatively high oxidase activity, and based on the product, the PS-CDs-MnO2-TMB system was designed to detect TPN with good linearity. This research provides a new perspective for the preparation of nanozymes and their applications in sensing, antibacterial, and biofilm growth inhibition fields.

Potassium permanganate (KMnO4), dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO), glucose and NaCl were purchased from Sinopharm Chemical Reagent Co. Ltd.. Protamine sulfates (PS) was purchased from Shanghai Yuanye Biotechnology Co. Ltd., tiopronin (TPN) was purchased from Dalian Meilun Biotechnology Co. Ltd., peptone (Oxoid Ltd) and yeast extract (Oxoid Ltd) were purchased from Basingstoke, Hampshire, England, 3,3',5,5'-Tetramethylbenzidine (TMB) and 3-(4,5-dimethylthiazol-2)-2,5-diphenyltetrazolium bromide salt (MTT) were purchased from Sigma-Aldrich. Staphylococcus aureus (S. aureus, CCTCC AB 91093), Bacillus subtilis (B. subtilis, CCTCC AB 90008), Escherichia coli (E. coli, CCTCC AB 93154) and Pseudomonas aeruginosa (P. aeruginosa, CCTCC AB 2013184) were purchased from the China Center for Type Culture Collection (CCTCC) in Wuhan, and other reagents such as PBS buffer (20 mM) and metal salts were analytically pure.

UV–Vis absorption spectra were obtained using a Lambda-35 UV–Vis spectrophotometer (Perkinlemer, Waltham, MA, USA) to determine the band gap absorption of PS-CDs-MnO2. FT-IR spectra were obtained on a Nicolet 6700 spectrometer (Somer Fisher Scientific, Waltham, MA, USA). JEM-2010F high-resolution field emission transmission electron microscope (Japan Electron Optics Laboratory Company). 101-3AB electric blast dryer (Tianjin Teste Instruments Co. Ltd.). SW-CJ-2FD clean bench (Suzhou Antai Air Technology Co. Ltd.). XFH-40CA electrothermal pressure steam sterilizer (Zhejiang Xinfeng (Medical Instruments Co. Ltd.). The optical density (OD) of the cells was measured in a microplate reader (Thermo Scientific, England, UK) at a wavelength of 600 nm. The Zeta potential of PS-CDs-MnO2 and PS-CDs was assessed using a Zetasizer (Nano ZS, Malvern Instruments, Worcestershire, UK). Scanning electron microscope (SEM) images for bacteria were conducted using Zeiss SIGMA scanning electron microscope (Carl Zeiss Jena).

Preparation of PS-CDs-MnO2: PS-CDs was obtained according to previous reports [39]. 40 mL of PS-CDs solution was taken with pH being adjusted to 4.5, and 500 μL 10 mM KMnO4 was added dropwise under stirring conditions. After stirring for 1 h, 300 μL 10 mM KMnO4 was added, and after stirring for 1 h, PS-CDs-MnO2 solution was obtained, and the resulting product was placed in a ultrafiltration tube (molecular weight: 3000) for 10 min, and the purified solid sample of PS-CDs-MnO2 was obtained by freeze-drying.

Colorimetric analysis procedure: the prepared PS-CDs-MnO2 (1.2 mg/mL) was immersed in 20 mM PBS (pH = 4.0) buffer and 100 μL of 1 mM TMB was added. After 8 min of reaction at room temperature, the TMB gradually changed from colorless to blue oxTMB. Subsequently, the blue color gradually disappeared with the addition of different concentrations of TPN. This color change was recorded by a UV–Vis spectrophotometer and quantified based on the characteristic absorption peak at 655 nm. The concentration of TPN can be determined by bringing the absorbance value into the calibration equation (in pure water or in the actual sample matrix). For possible interfering ions, such as Mn2+, Fe3+, and K+, a similar procedure was used, except that the concentration of the interfering ion was kept constant at 1 mM.

ce:section.ce:section-title: The synthesis and characterization of PS-CDs-MnO2
ce:section.ce:para: With PS-CDs (prepared through one-step hydrothermal route reported by our lab) [39] as reducing agent, the MnO2 nanozyme has been prepared by reducing KMnO4 through stirring under room temperature. After the addition of colorless PC-CDs solution into KMnO4, the solution gradually changes from purplish red to brownish yellow, indicating that PS-CDs could reduce KMnO4 into MnO2 (see Scheme 1 ). Meanwhile, PS-CDs could also work as surface stabilizer to avoid the aggregation of PS-CDs-MnO2 in pure water solution. The prepared PS-CDs-MnO2 nanozyme exhibits obvious merits like green and simple synthesis route with low energy cost, which is in accordance to the Environment-friendly chemistry. Considering the mass production in the future commercial-production-industry application. When scaling up the raw material at 10 times, the properties of the product still remain the same. Meanwhile, the synthesis operation of PS-CDs-MnO2 nanozyme is simple, raw materials are easy to acquire, and its solid state sample can be preserved at least 2 months, beneficial to the large scale application in mass production, time–cost saving, and sustainable development.

The catalytic oxidation TMB color development tests were used to examine whether PS-CDs-MnO2 possesses oxidase activity. As shown in Fig. 1 (a), the control group (TMB alone) does not have UV absorption peak at 655 nm, and the solution color does not change; while after the addition of PS-CDs-MnO2, even without the presence of H2O2, TMB could be catalyzed into oxTMB, producing an obvious color reaction and obvious UV absorption peak at 655 nm. This proves the oxidase property of PS-CDs-MnO2. The control group PS-CDs could not catalyze TMB, the solution color does not exhibit any change, and there is no absorption at 655 nm. The experiments prove the successful preparation of PS-CDs-MnO2 nanozyme with oxidase activity.

The morphology, surface charge, and element content of prepared oxidase PS-CDs-MnO2 were investigated by a series of characterization means, including TEM, Zeta potential, and UV–Vis. TEM has been used to study the morphology of PS-CDs-MnO2. The average particle size is 21 nm (Fig. S1(a)). As shown in Fig. 1(b), the dark-colored particles dope among light-colored structure. The dark dots of them are believed to be MnO2 particles and the light dots are PS-CDs. UV–Vis spectroscopy was used to evaluate the optical property of prepared PS-CDs-MnO2 (Fig. 1(a)). The UV–Vis spectrum of MnO2 usually possesses a broad absorption band (300–500 nm) with characteristic absorption at 380 nm, which is in accordance with the published literature [40], further proving the successful preparation of nanozyme PS-CDs-MnO2. EDS is a method for semi-quantitative elemental analysis with simple operation, fast analysis speed, and direct results. As shown from the EDS image of PS-CDs-MnO2 (Fig. 1(c)), the elements in PS-CDs-MnO2 are C, N, O, S, and Mn with percentages at 39.776%, 24.250%, 29.450%, 4.180%, 2.343% respectively, further proving that the nanozyme PS-CDs-MnO2 has been successfully prepared. FTIR was used to study the basic elements and chemical structures of prepared PS-CDs-MnO2 and PS-CDs. As shown in Fig. S1(b), the FTIR spectra of PS-CDs-MnO2 and PS-CDs are almost identical, showing that PS-CDs confer some groups to PS-CDs-MnO2. They have the same absorption peaks at 3343, 3177, 1659 and 1087 cm−1, representing NH/OH, CH2, CN and CH groups respectively. The rich surface groups on PS-CDs-MnO2 and PS-CDs make them positively charged in aqueous solution, giving them great advantages in antibacterial applications.

In addition, MTT assays were used to examine the biocompatibility of PS-CDs-MnO2 or PS-CDs with L-929 mouse connective tissue cells for in vitro cytotoxicity tests. As shown in Fig. 1(d), when the concentrations of PS-CDs-MnO2 and PS-CDs are lower than 200 μg/mL, the average cell survival rates are higher than 80% and 74% respectively, proving good biocompatibility and can be applied in biochemical analysis and antibacterial fields.

ce:section.ce:section-title: The study on the antibacterial and biofilm-inhibiting ability of PS-CDs-MnO2
The study on the antibacterial ability of PS-CDs-MnO2
ce:section.ce:para: To examine the antibacterial ability of prepared nanozyme PS-CDs-MnO2, S. aureus were used as a model bacterial for antibacterial tests. The results showed that PS-CDs-MnO2 inherits the antibacterial ability from PS-CDs. The impacts of pH value upon the antibacterial ability of prepared PS-CDs-MnO2 by adjusting the pH of the medium. As shown in Table 2 , PS-CDs-MnO2 exhibits stronger antibacterial ability in an alkaline environment than in an acidic environment. It has the best antibacterial ability at pH = 8.5. However, the control group shows that even without the presence of PS-CDs-MnO2, the alkaline environment could also inhibit the growth of S. aureus, so the pH was set at 8 in the subsequent antibacterial tests. Since the pH value in the physiological system tends to exceed 7.0 (even 8.0 in chronic wounds), the development of antibacterial nanozyme in an alkaline environment is of great significance.

ce:section.ce:section-title: MICs against different strains of bacteria
ce:section.ce:para: A variety of G+ and G− bacteria were selected to investigate the antibacterial ability of PS-CDs-MnO2. The above experimental results show that the antibacterial ability of PS-CDs-MnO2 is stronger in the environment of pH = 8, so all these experiments were conducted at pH = 8. As shown in Table 3 , PS-CDs-MnO2 has broad-spectrum antibacterial ability, but its are inhibiting abilities against different bacteria vary. At the same time, the antibacterial ability of prepared PS-CDs-MnO2 have been compared with those of other MnO2 antibacterial nanomaterials, as shown in Table 1, the prepared nanozyme has exhibited obvious merits over other MnO2 nanomaterials in both inhibiting time and antibacterial effect.

ce:section.ce:section-title: The long-term antibacterial ability of PS-CDs-MnO2 against S. aureus
ce:section.ce:para: PS-CDs-MnO2 exhibits an obvious advantage in long-term antibacterial activity. As shown in Fig. 2 (b), under the concentration of 7.5 μg/mL, the antibacterial ability of PS-CDs against S. aureus lasts 20 h (Fig. 2(a)), while under the same concentration, the antibacterial ability of PS-CDs-MnO2 lasts 48 h. At the concentration of 5 μg/mL, PS-CDs-MnO2 shows antibacterial ability for 10 h, and then its ability weakens, and S. aureus shows signs of slow growth. In contrast, at the same concentration, PS-CDs could also be antibacterial for 10 h, but S. aureus grow rapidly after that. The antibacterial time of most MnO2 nanomaterials lasts for overnight or 24 h, but the prepared PS-CDs-MnO2 still exhibit antibacterial effect in 48 h [35–38].

ce:section.ce:section-title: The inhibition ability of PS-CDs-MnO2 against S. aureus biofilm growth
ce:section.ce:para: As shown in Fig. 3 (a), the minimum inhibitory concentration (MBIC) of PS-CDs-MnO2 against the growth of S. aureus biofilm at pH = 7.0 is 40 μg/mL, and is 10 μg/mL when pH value is at 8.0 (Fig. 3(c)), as for PS-CDs, its MBICs against S. aureus biofilm are 80 μg/mL and 40 μg/mL while the pH values are at 7.0 and 8.0 respectively (Fig. 3(b) and (d)), showing stronger inhibition ability of PS-CDs-MnO2 than that of PS-CDs. Both PS-CDs and PS-CDs-MnO2 exhibit stronger inhibition ability at pH = 8 than pH = 7, which is in accordance with the results acquired in previous experiments. For the present, the reported MnO2 nanozyme requires high concentration (8 mM) when used to inhibit the growth of S. aureus biofilm growth, and could only inhibit 80% formation of bacterial biofilm [41]. However, the prepared nanozyme PS-CDs-MnO2 could completely inhibit its growth at the concentration of 10 μg/mL, showing great potential in inhibiting bacterial biofilm growth.

The long-term high antibacterial ability might originate from the leakage of Mn2+ with antibacterial activity [44] during their incubation with bacteria. The catalytic TMB oxidation discoloration experiments after the incubation of S. aureus with the presence of PS-CDs-MnO2 have been used to verify the speculation. S. aureus were incubated with PS-CDs-MnO2 for 0, 1, 2, 4, 6, 8 and 20 h, and then buffer solution (pH = 4) and TMB were added into the mixed solution, and the absorbance at 655 nm was acquired through UV–Vis. As shown in Fig. 5 , with the increase in incubation time, the absorption gradually decreases, indicating that S. aureus can ablate PS-CDs-MnO2 and reduce the catalytic activity of the nanozyme.

A highly-selective and sensitive colorimetric method based on the oxidase activity of PS-CDs-MnO2 was developed for the detection of TPN. The present researches show that glutathione (GSH) can decompose manganese dioxide into Mn2+, and remove the hydrogen on the sulfhydryl group of GSH to turn it into GSSG. Meanwhile, it can reduce oxTMB into colorless TMB [45]. TPN is a sulfhydryl-containing derivative with two reactive groups, sulfhydryl group and glycine group, and which is similar to that of GSH. Based on this, the PS-CDs-MnO2-TMB colorimetric sensing system was designed and developed for the detection of TPN. Fig. 6 shows the change in optical properties of PS-CDs-MnO2 and TMB solution before and after the addition of TPN. The addition of PS-CDs-MnO2 as an oxidase could oxidize TMB and there appears an absorption peak at 655 nm. On the contrary, after the addition of TPN, the absorption peak at 655 nm decreases significantly, and color of the solution becomes lighter. It was speculated that TPN could decompose PS-CDs-MnO2 into Mn2+, and reduce the activity of PS-CDs-MnO2 as oxidase, leading to a lower efficiency of oxidation of TMB into oxTMB.

ce:section.ce:section-title: The pH-dependency of enzyme activity of PS-CDs-MnO2
ce:section.ce:para: To further optimize the sensitive rapid detection of TPN based on PS-CDs-MnO2, the absorbance changes under various experimental changes were investigated. The catalytic activity of PS-CDs-MnO2 depends on pH value of buffer, the reaction time, and the amounts of TMB and PS-CDs-MnO2. By adjusting the above parameters, it was found that, the change rate of absorbance is the greatest when the amount of TMB is 100 μL (Fig. S3(a)). The impacts of pH value (3.0–5.0) upon the catalytic reaction of PS-CDs-MnO2 with 20 mM PBS as buffer were investigated. As shown in Fig. S3(b) and Fig. S3(c), the change rate of absorbance at 655 nm is the greatest when the pH = 4, and it keeps stable when the reaction time is at 8 min. With the increase of PS-CDs-MnO2 amount, the absorbance gradually increases, and the change reaches the greatest when the amount is 60 μL (Fig. S3(d)). The detection environment was adjusted accordingly.

The sensitivity and selectivity of the PS-CDs-MnO2 to TPN were investigated under optimal conditions. Fig. 7 (a) shows that after the addition of TPN into TMB solution catalyzed by PS-CDs-MnO2, the absorbance of the solution gradually decreases, and the solution color also becomes lighter and lighter (inset of Fig. 7). Fig. 7(b) shows the linear relationship between the absorbance (A) of the mixed solution after the addition of TPN and its concentration ranging from 285.71 nM to 14.28 μM at 655 nm. The equation is A655 = −0.0249 CTPN + 0.3398 with regression coefficient (R2) at 0.9945. LOD of TPN is defined as the three times of relative standard deviation (RSD) of eleven times of tests after the addition of TPN at the minimum concentration, and the confirmed amount is 6.30 nM. Yao et al. used prepared GQDs as fluorescence probe to detect the presence of TPN, with acquired detection range at 0.01–0.4 μM and detection limit at 1.25 nM [31]. However, PS-CDs-MnO2-TMB colorimetric sensing system possesses wider detection range. As shown in Fig. 8 , the additions of other common ions (K+, Mn2+, Pb2+, Na+, Cu2+, Zn2 +, Mg2+, Ag+, Ca2+, Hg2+ and Fe3+) and Glc have no obvious effect upon the detection system, indicating the excellent selectivity of the system.

This paper reports the rapid and facile preparation of PS-CDs-MnO2 nanozyme with oxidase activity using PS-CDs as reducing agent to reduce KMnO4. Various characterization means have been used to study the element content and surface properties, including UV–Vis, FTIR, EDS and Zeta potential tests. The prepared PS-CDs-MnO2 exhibited antibacterial and inhibition of biofilm growth activity due to the destruction of 1O2 and positive charge attraction. Its MIC against S. aureus is 7.50 μg/mL under alkaline conditions, with long-term antibacterial ability, and it also exhibits highly efficient inhibitory ability against the biofilm growth with MBIC against S. aureus biofilm at 10 μg/mL, showing its great potential in antibacterial fields. Meanwhile, the PS-CDs-MnO2-TMB system has been developed to realize the sensitive and specific colorimetric detection of TPN based on the effective competitiveness of TPN with TMB, with a detection range at 285.71 nM–14.28 μM, and detection limit at 6.30 nM. This research provides a fast and simple preparation method of oxidase nanozyme and expands its application in drug detection and biomedical fields.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was funded by the Fundamental Research Funds for South-Central Minzu University (CZY22003) and the National Natural Science Foundation of China (2197832).
